basic_details.job_title: IOCL AOD Digboi Refinery Apprentice Recruitment 2026: Apply Online for 126 Posts
basic_details.selection_process_brief: Merit list based on qualifying marks (No written test)
basic_details.short_information: Indian Oil Corporation Limited (IOCL), AOD Digboi Refinery has released dated 07.07.2026 inviting online applications for engagement of 126 Trade and Technician Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961/1973. Eligible candidates with the required qualification in Chemical, Mechanical, Electrical, Instrumentation, Secretarial, Accounts and Data Entry Operator streams can apply through the NAPS/NATS portal from 08.07.2026 to 31.07.2026.

application_fee.details: The official notification does not mention any application fee for any category of candidates.

age_limit.details: The Marksheet/Certificate issued by the Board of Secondary Education for Class X (Matriculation) is the only document accepted as proof of age.

qualification.details: Candidates must satisfy the nationality/eligibility criteria as prescribed under the Apprentices Act, 1961/1973 and applicable Government of India guidelines. The notification does not specify a separate nationality clause beyond statutory eligibility under the Act. | Code 101/103: 3-year B.Sc. with Maths, Physics, Chemistry or Industrial Chemistry. Code 102: Matriculation with ITI in Fitter Trade (minimum 2 years duration) with Pass class. Code 104: 3-year Diploma in Chemical Engg./Petrochemical Engg./Chemical Technology/Refinery and Petrochemical Engg. Code 105: 3-year Diploma in Mechanical Engg. Code 106: 3-year Diploma in Electrical Engg. or Electrical and Electronics Engg. Code 107: 3-year Diploma in Instrumentation Engg./Instrumentation & Electronics/Instrumentation & Control Engg./Applied Electronics and Instrumentation Engg. Code 108: 3-year B.A./B.Sc/B.Com. Code 109: 3-year B.Com. Code 110: Class XII pass. Code 111: Class XII pass with Skill Certificate holder in "Domestic Data Entry Operator." | Minimum 50% aggregate marks are required (45% for SC/ST/PwBD candidates against reserved seats), and the qualification must be from a recognized Board/University/Institute through a regular full-time course. Distance/correspondence qualifications are not eligible. A candidate with B.Sc. (Physics, Maths, Chemistry/Industrial Chemistry) can apply for only one of Code 101 or Code 103. Candidates applying for more than one trade/discipline will be summarily rejected. | Candidates whose qualifying exam result is awaited, who hold higher qualifications (BE/B.Tech, MBA, CA, LLB, MCA or equivalent), who have relevant job experience of 1 year or more after acquiring the qualification, or who completed the qualification more than 5 years before the eligibility date are not eligible.

3-year Diploma in Electrical Engg./Electrical & Electronics Engg.
3-year Diploma in Instrumentation Engg./Instrumentation & Electronics/Instrumentation & Control Engg./Applied Electronics & Instrumentation Engg.

salary_details.details: The rate of stipend payable to apprentices is as prescribed under the Apprentices Act, 1961/1973, Apprentices Rules 1992 (as amended), and IOCL's guidelines. The specific stipend amount is not mentioned in this notification. | Personal Protective Equipment: Apprentices under Codes 101–107 will be provided one pair of safety shoes and a helmet (returnable on completion of training). Accommodation/HRA: No HRA or Company accommodation will be provided. Leave: 32 days General Leave (8 days/quarter) and 12 days Casual Leave per calendar year (pro-rata); unavailed leave lapses at the end of the training period. Insurance: Apprentices are covered under Accident Insurance. Employment obligation: IOCL has no obligation to offer employment after completion of apprenticeship, and apprentices cannot claim employment rights on this basis.

selection_process: There is no written test for this recruitment. Selection is purely merit-based:
A merit list is prepared based on the percentage of marks (aggregate of all subjects across all years/semesters) obtained in the prescribed qualification
in descending order. Preference is given to candidates applying for apprenticeship training for the first time. In case of a tie in marks
the candidate senior in age (date of birth) is ranked higher; if the date of birth is also the same

higher percentage in Matriculation is considered. PwBD candidates who avail relaxation in qualifying marks are considered against reserved seats based on merit within their category. Shortlisted candidates are called for Document Verification
followed by pre-engagement medical fitness examination.
how_to_apply: The application process has two mandatory steps. Both must be completed for the application to be considered.

Codes 101, 102, 103, 108, 109, 110, 111: Register and apply on the NAPS portal at apprenticeshipindia.gov.in. Search for the opportunity using the IOCL Refinery Unit location and select the respective trade.
Codes 104, 105, 106, 107: Register on the NATS/Board of Apprenticeship Training (BOAT) portal at nats.education.gov.in and apply to the notification on the dashboard.
After applying on NAPS/NATS, candidates must mandatorily submit additional details via Microsoft Forms at: https://forms.office.com/r/g0ZP2igeTa. Only one response per candidate is allowed.

Candidates must ensure the Enrollment/Student ID/Registration No. and other details match exactly between Step I and Step II. The same e-mail ID and mobile number used on NAPS/NATS must be used and kept valid for the entire apprenticeship period. Incomplete applications, or those not meeting eligibility criteria, will be treated as "Rejected."
important_instructions: Candidates can apply for only one trade/discipline of their choice. Furnishing false or incorrect information at any stage will lead to cancellation of candidature and possible criminal prosecution. SC/ST/PwBD candidates attending Document Verification will be reimbursed single second-class railway fare (if distance is 30 km or more) from the nearest railway station to the venue and back. Any corrigendum/addendum related to this recruitment will be published only on www.iocl.com. Canvassing in any form will make a candidate ineligible. Disputes are subject to the jurisdiction of the local court at Digboi. IOCL has cautioned candidates to be aware of fraudulent job offers and to rely only on information from www.iocl.com and official Employment News/Press Notifications.

content_sections.heading: Official Notification
content_sections.text: Candidates are advised to read the detailed official notification before applying. The notification and application links are available on the official IOCL website: www.iocl.com under "What's New" > Engagement of Apprentices under AOD Digboi Refinery Unit.

content_sections.heading: IOCL Digboi Apprentice Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: A total of 126 seats have been notified across 11 trade/discipline codes. The seat numbers indicated are tentative and may vary at the discretion of the management. Note: PwBD candidates can apply only against seats identified under Codes 105, 106, 107, 108 and 110.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates must satisfy the nationality/eligibility criteria as prescribed under the Apprentices Act, 1961/1973 and applicable Government of India guidelines. The notification does not specify a separate nationality clause beyond statutory eligibility under the Act. Code 101/103: 3-year B.Sc. with Maths, Physics, Chemistry or Industrial Chemistry. Code 102: Matriculation with ITI in Fitter Trade (minimum 2 years duration) with Pass class. Code 104: 3-year Diploma in Chemical Engg./Petrochemical Engg./Chemical Technology/Refinery and Petrochemical Engg. Code 105: 3-year Diploma in Mechanical Engg. Code 106: 3-year Diploma in Electrical Engg. or Electrical and Electronics Engg. Code 107: 3-year Diploma in Instrumentation Engg./Instrumentation & Electronics/Instrumentation & Control Engg./Applied Electronics and Instrumentation Engg. Code 108: 3-year B.A./B.Sc/B.Com. Code 109: 3-year B.Com. Code 110: Class XII pass. Code 111: Class XII pass with Skill Certificate holder in "Domestic Data Entry Operator." Minimum 50% aggregate marks are required (45% for SC/ST/PwBD candidates against reserved seats), and the qualification must be from a recognized Board/University/Institute through a regular full-time course. Distance/correspondence qualifications are not eligible . A candidate with B.Sc. (Physics, Maths, Chemistry/Industrial Chemistry) can apply for only one of Code 101 or Code 103. Candidates applying for more than one trade/discipline will be summarily rejected. Candidates whose qualifying exam result is awaited, who hold higher qualifications (BE/B.Tech, MBA, CA, LLB, MCA or equivalent), who have relevant job experience of 1 year or more after acquiring the qualification, or who completed the qualification more than 5 years before the eligibility date are not eligible .

content_sections.heading: Age Limit
content_sections.text: The Marksheet/Certificate issued by the Board of Secondary Education for Class X (Matriculation) is the only document accepted as proof of age.

rows.age_relaxation: Minimum 18 years, Maximum 24 years as on 30.06.2026
Up to 5 years relaxation for reserved positions
Up to 3 years relaxation for reserved positions
Up to 10 years relaxation (irrespective of reservation)

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: The official notification does not mention any application fee for any category of candidates.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: There is no written test for this recruitment. Selection is purely merit-based: A merit list is prepared based on the percentage of marks (aggregate of all subjects across all years/semesters) obtained in the prescribed qualification, in descending order. Preference is given to candidates applying for apprenticeship training for the first time. In case of a tie in marks, the candidate senior in age (date of birth) is ranked higher; if the date of birth is also the same, higher percentage in Matriculation is considered. PwBD candidates who avail relaxation in qualifying marks are considered against reserved seats based on merit within their category. Shortlisted candidates are called for Document Verification, followed by pre-engagement medical fitness examination.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required at Document Verification
content_sections.text: 10th Pass/Matriculation certificate (proof of date of birth) Class XII marksheet / Semester-wise ITI (Fitter) marksheets / Diploma / Graduation marksheets, as applicable Final Class XII / ITI (Fitter) / Diploma / Graduation certificate CGPA/OGPA/Letter Grade to percentage conversion certificate, if applicable Certificate from Principal/Institute confirming date of result publication, regular full-time mode, and good conduct Category certificate: SC/ST/OBC (NCL) with declaration, Disability Certificate, or EWS Income & Asset Certificate, as applicable Skill Certificate in "Domestic Data Entry Operator" (for Code 111 candidates only) Printout of NATS/NAPS portal registration with applicant details Affidavit as per Annexure-A confirming compliance with apprenticeship eligibility conditions One original Photo ID proof (Aadhar Card/PAN Card/Voter ID/Driving License/Passport)

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for IOCL Digboi Apprentice Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: The application process has two mandatory steps . Both must be completed for the application to be considered. Codes 101, 102, 103, 108, 109, 110, 111: Register and apply on the NAPS portal at apprenticeshipindia.gov.in . Search for the opportunity using the IOCL Refinery Unit location and select the respective trade. Codes 104, 105, 106, 107: Register on the NATS/Board of Apprenticeship Training (BOAT) portal at nats.education.gov.in and apply to the notification on the dashboard. After applying on NAPS/NATS, candidates must mandatorily submit additional details via Microsoft Forms at: https://forms.office.com/r/g0ZP2igeTa . Only one response per candidate is allowed. Candidates must ensure the Enrollment/Student ID/Registration No. and other details match exactly between Step I and Step II. The same e-mail ID and mobile number used on NAPS/NATS must be used and kept valid for the entire apprenticeship period. Incomplete applications, or those not meeting eligibility criteria, will be treated as "Rejected."

content_sections.heading: Stipend and Other Benefits
content_sections.text: The rate of stipend payable to apprentices is as prescribed under the Apprentices Act, 1961/1973, Apprentices Rules 1992 (as amended), and IOCL's guidelines. The specific stipend amount is not mentioned in this notification. Personal Protective Equipment: Apprentices under Codes 101–107 will be provided one pair of safety shoes and a helmet (returnable on completion of training). Accommodation/HRA: No HRA or Company accommodation will be provided. Leave: 32 days General Leave (8 days/quarter) and 12 days Casual Leave per calendar year (pro-rata); unavailed leave lapses at the end of the training period. Insurance: Apprentices are covered under Accident Insurance. Employment obligation: IOCL has no obligation to offer employment after completion of apprenticeship, and apprentices cannot claim employment rights on this basis.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Candidates can apply for only one trade/discipline of their choice. Furnishing false or incorrect information at any stage will lead to cancellation of candidature and possible criminal prosecution. SC/ST/PwBD candidates attending Document Verification will be reimbursed single second-class railway fare (if distance is 30 km or more) from the nearest railway station to the venue and back. Any corrigendum/addendum related to this recruitment will be published only on www.iocl.com . Canvassing in any form will make a candidate ineligible. Disputes are subject to the jurisdiction of the local court at Digboi. IOCL has cautioned candidates to be aware of fraudulent job offers and to rely only on information from www.iocl.com and official Employment News/Press Notifications.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in IOCL Digboi Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are a total of 126 apprentice vacancies across 11 trade and technician disciplines, including Chemical, Mechanical, Electrical, Instrumentation, Secretarial Assistant, Accountant, and Data Entry Operator.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for IOCL Digboi Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application on the NAPS/NATS portal and Microsoft Forms is 31.07.2026 up to 17:00 Hrs.

faq.question: Q3. Is there a written exam for IOCL Apprentice Recruitment?
faq.answer: No. There is no written test. Selection is based purely on merit - the percentage of marks obtained in the qualifying examination.

faq.question: Q4. Can ITI Fitter candidates apply for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Yes. Candidates with Matriculation and ITI in Fitter Trade (minimum 2 years, Pass class) can apply under Code 102 – Trade Apprentice (Fitter).

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for IOCL Digboi Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 24 years as on 30.06.2026 for General/EWS candidates, with relaxation applicable for SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/PwBD candidates.

faq.question: Q6. Is there an application fee for IOCL Apprentice Recruitment?
faq.answer: The official notification does not mention any application fee.

faq.question: Q7. What is the stipend for IOCL apprentices?
faq.answer: The stipend is paid as per the rates prescribed under the Apprentices Act, 1961/1973 and Apprentices Rules, 1992. The exact stipend amount is not specified in this notification.

faq.question: Q8. Which portal should I use to apply - NAPS or NATS?
faq.answer: Candidates applying for Codes 101, 102, 103, 108, 109, 110, and 111 must use the NAPS portal (apprenticeshipindia.gov.in). Candidates applying for Codes 104, 105, 106, and 107 must use the NATS/BOAT portal (nats.education.gov.in).

faq.question: Q9. Can a candidate apply for more than one trade or discipline?
faq.answer: No. A candidate can apply for only one trade/discipline. Applications for multiple trades/disciplines will be summarily rejected.

faq.question: Q10. Does completing this apprenticeship guarantee a job at IOCL?
faq.answer: No. IOCL has clarified that there is no obligation to offer employment after completion of the apprenticeship period, and apprentices cannot claim employment rights on this basis.
